manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3207

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A19": 67, "B91": 45, "A30": 308, "B11": 835, "C47": 819, "C75": 667, "C26": 547, "C73": 322, "A47": 428, "A15": 306, "A75": 327, "A39": 362, "A56": 280, "C24": 334, "B26": 767}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A19": 138, "B91": 583, "A30": 822, "B11": 783, "C47": 65, "C75": 262, "C26": 121, "C73": 508, "A47": 780, "A15": 461, "A75": 484, "A39": 668, "A56": 389, "C24": 808, "B26": 215}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.

message.content:
PROPOSAL: {'A19', 'B91', 'A30', 'B11', 'C24', 'C73', 'A47', 'A75', 'A39'}
ARGUMENT: {'This proposal selects a combination of items that optimally utilizes the available effort budget to cover the most critical aspects of the problem.'}
message.reasoning:
The user wants me to act as a participant in a collaborative negotiation game.
The goal is to agree on a set of items with another participant.
I have a list of items with their effort values and my personal importance values.
There is a shared effort limit: LIMIT = 3207.

Data:
Item Effort: {"A19": 67, "B91": 45, "A30": 308, "B11": 835, "C47": 819, "C75": 667, "C26": 547, "C73": 322, "A47": 428, "A15": 306, "A75": 327, "A39": 362, "A56": 280, "C24": 334, "B26": 767}
My Importance: {"A19": 138, "B91": 583, "A30": 822, "B11": 783, "C47": 65, "C75": 262, "C26": 121, "C73": 508, "A47": 780, "A15": 461, "A75": 484, "A39": 668, "A56": 389, "C24": 808, "B26": 215}

Constraints:
1. Agree on a set of items.
2. Total effort <= 3207.
3. Maximize my total importance.
4. I don't know the opponent's values.
5. I must use specific tags: PROPOSAL, REFUSE, ARGUMENT, AGREE.
6. I must include ARGUMENT at least once in every message.
7. Format: TAG: {...}
8. Do not reveal importance scores.
9. Game ends with AGREE on an active proposal.

Strategy:
I need to find a high-value set for myself under the limit to propose.
But I also need to be prepared to negotiate because the opponent wants to maximize their value.

Let's analyze my values vs effort (Value per effort ratio) to see what I really want.
Items:
A19: Eff=67, Val=138. Ratio ~2.06
B91: Eff=45, Val=583. Ratio ~12.95 (Very high)
A30: Eff=308, Val=822. Ratio ~2.67
B11: Eff=835, Val=783. Ratio ~0.94 (Low)
C47: Eff=819, Val=65. Ratio ~0.08 (Terrible)
C75: Eff=667, Val=262. Ratio ~0.39
C26: Eff=547, Val=121. Ratio ~0.22
C73: Eff=322, Val=508. Ratio ~1.58
A47: Eff=428, Val=780. Ratio ~1.82
A15: Eff=306, Val=461. Ratio ~1.51
A75: Eff=327, Val=484. Ratio ~1.48
A39: Eff=362, Val=668. Ratio ~1.85
A56: Eff=280, Val=389. Ratio ~1.39
C24: Eff=334, Val=808. Ratio ~2.42
B26: Eff=767, Val=215. Ratio ~0.28

Total Effort of all items:
67+45+308+835+819+667+547+322+428+306+327+362+280+334+767
Let's sum:
67+45=112
112+308=420
420+835=1255
1255+819=2074
2074+667=2741
2741+547=3288 (Already over limit 3207, so we have to drop some expensive low-value items)

Items I definitely want to keep (high value/ratio):
B91 (583), A30 (822), C24 (808), A47 (780), A39 (668).
Effort of these: 45 + 308 + 334 + 428 + 362 = 1477.
Remaining Limit: 3207 - 1477 = 1730.

Items I want to try to keep:
B11 (783), A15 (461), A75 (484), C73 (508), A56 (389), A19 (138).
Let's sort remaining by value:
B11 (835 eff, 783 val) - high value but high effort.
C73 (322 eff, 508 val).
A15 (306 eff, 461 val).
A75 (327 eff, 484 val).
A56 (280 eff, 389 val).
A19 (67 eff, 138 val).

If I add B11 (835), C73 (322), A15 (306), A75 (327).
Effort added: 835 + 322 + 306 + 327 = 1790.
Total effort: 1477 + 1790 = 3267 > 3207. Too much.

Let's try a robust set.
Core: B91(45), A30(308), C24(334), A47(428), A39(362). Total Eff: 1477. Total Val: 3661.
Remaining budget: 1730.

Combinations to fit 1730:
Option 1: B11 (835) + C73 (322) + A75 (327) + A56 (280). Eff = 835+322+327+280 = 1764 (Close, over).
Drop A56 (280). Eff = 1484. Total = 1477 + 1484 = 2961.
Items: B91, A30, C24, A47, A39, B11, C73, A75.
Val: 583+822+808+780+668+783+508+484 = 5436.
Effort: 2961. Limit: 3207. Margin: 246.
Can I fit A19 (67)? Yes. Effort 3028.
Set: {A19, B91, A30, B11, C24, C73, A47, A75, A39}.
Value: 5436 + 138 = 5574.
Effort: 3028.

Let's try to fit A15 instead of A19.
A15 Eff=306.
Current Eff=3028 - 67 + 306 = 3267. Too high.
So stick with A19.

What about A56 (280)?
Replace A19 (67) with A56 (280)? Effort = 3028 - 67 + 280 = 3241. Too high.

So the candidate set is {A19, B91, A30, B11, C24, C73, A47, A75, A39}.
Let's double check sum:
67+45+308+835+334+322+428+327+362 =
67+45=112
112+308=420
420+835=1255
1255+334=1589
1589+322=1911
1911+428=2339
2339+327=2666
2666+362=3028.
3028 <= 3207.

Items to exclude:
C47 (819), C75 (667), C26 (547), B26 (767). These are all very high effort, low value for me.

This looks like a good starting proposal. It maximizes my high value items. The opponent might want some of the items I excluded (C47, C75, C26, B26) or some of the ones I included.
Since I don't know their values, I propose my optimal set.

I must include an ARGUMENT.
Argument strategy: Emphasize efficiency or utility without revealing numbers. "This selection offers a balanced distribution of high-potential items within the constraints." Or something generic but plausible.

Message Structure:
PROPOSAL: {'A19', 'B91', 'A30', 'B11', 'C24', 'C73', 'A47', 'A75', 'A39'}
ARGUMENT: {'This set provides a robust collection of items that make efficient use of the available effort capacity.'}

Wait, the prompt says "You are participating...". The other participant is the user (or the environment acting as the other). The prompt implies I should respond to a turn, but this is the start? Or should I initiate?
"Interaction Protocol: ... You may only use the following structured formats in a message: PROPOSAL..."
The prompt says "You are participating...". Usually, I should start or respond if there was context. There is no previous context. So I should make the first move (Proposal).

Let's refine the Argument to be persuasive. "I believe this combination covers a wide range of key interests while respecting the effort limit perfectly."

Let's verify the list of items again.
My set: A19, B91, A30, B11, C24, C73, A47, A75, A39.
Effort: 67+45+308+835+334+322+428+327+362 = 3028. Correct.

Wait, is there any reason to leave room?
Maybe proposing a slightly lower effort set allows for negotiation.
But I need to maximize my value. 3028 is close to 3207.
If I propose the max, I can't add anything, only swap.
If I swap, I lose value.
So proposing the max set that I like is the right opening move.

One check: Item names.
A19, B91, A30, B11, C47, C75, C26, C73, A47, A15, A75, A39, A56, C24, B26.
My selection: A19, B91, A30, B11, C24, C73, A47, A75, A39.
Missing: C47, C75, C26, A15, A56, B26.

A15 (461 val, 306 eff) vs A19 (138 val, 67 eff).
If I replace A19 with A15, Eff = 3028 - 67 + 306 = 3267. Over.
A56 (389 val, 280 eff). Replace A19 with A56 -> 3241. Over.
So A19 is a filler item because it's cheap.

What about C73 (508 val, 322 eff)?
If I drop C73, I free 322.
Then I could add A15 (306) and A56 (280)? 306+280=586. 586 > 322. No.
Drop C73 (322). Add A15 (306). Net loss of 16 effort.
Current Eff: 3028 - 322 + 306 = 3012.
Value change: 508 -> 461. Loss of 47.
So the set with C73 is better.

What about B11 (783 val, 835 eff)?
Very heavy.
Drop B11 (835).
Add C26 (547) + C73 (322)? 547+322 = 869. Close, but slightly more effort.
Add C26 (547) + A56 (280)? 547+280 = 827. Fits (slightly less).
Value: 783 -> 121 + 389 = 510. Bad trade.
Add B26 (767) + A15 (306)? 767+306 = 1073. Too much.
Add B26 (767)? Eff 3028 - 835 + 767 = 2960.
Value: 783 -> 215. Bad trade.
So B11 is essential for me despite high effort.

So the set seems optimal for me.

Final check of format.
"Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure."
The content in my response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty. (It is).
Only use TAG: {...} format.
Must include ARGUMENT at least once.

I will construct the message now.
